label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out if the sentence "Max Scherzer was safe at first." is plausible. Let me start by recalling who Max Scherzer is. He's a professional baseball player, right? I remember he's a pitcher for the New York Mets. So, in baseball, when a player is "safe at first," that usually means he reached first base without being out. Typically, a batter reaches first base by hitting a ball and not getting thrown out, or by a walk, or maybe an error. But pitchers don't usually get to first base because their main role is to pitch, not to bat. In most games, the pitcher is the last batter in the lineup and doesn't get many at-bats. But wait, sometimes pitchers do bat, especially in certain situations. For example, if the team is ahead and the pitcher is the only one left in the lineup, they might have to bat. Also, in some cases, a pitcher might be hit by a pitch, which would put them on first base. If that happens, they are safe because a hit-by-pitch is considered a walk. So, if Max Scherzer was hit by a pitch, he would be safe at first. That scenario is possible. Another way a pitcher could be safe
